name: Caramel-Filled Butter Pecan Cake - Dkp
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup butter or margarine, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
COCOA PASTRY DOUGH:
1. Cream the shortening with an electric mixer on medium speed.
2. Gradually add sugar, beating well after each addition of dry ingredients.
3. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each egg is incorporated.
4. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt; alternate adding to the creamed mixture with milk, beginning and ending with the flour mixture. Mix thoroughly after each addition.
5. Stir in almond extract and vanilla extract.
6. Grease three 9-inch round cake pans and line the bottoms with wax paper. Pour batter into prepared pans.
7. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 22 to 25 minutes, or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
8. Cool in the pans for 10 minutes before removing the cakes. Transfer them to wire racks and cool completely.
CARMEL FILLING:
1. In a heavy saucepan over medium heat, sprinkle half of the sugar.
2. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sugar melts and the syrup is light golden brown.
3. Combine the remaining sugar, milk, beaten egg, and salt in a bowl; stir well.
4. Stir in butter into the mixture.
5. Pour the butter mixture into the hot caramelized sugar and continue cooking over medium heat while stirring frequently.
6. Cook until a candy thermometer registers 230°F (115°C), about 15 to 20 minutes.
7. Let cool for 5 minutes, then beat with a wooden spoon until it reaches almost a spreading consistency.
BUTTERCREAM FROSTING:
1. Cream the butter or margarine until smooth on medium speed of an electric mixer.
2. Gradually add powdered sugar and half and half (or milk), beating until light and fluffy.
3. Stir in vanilla extract.
ASSEMBLY:
1. Spread a layer of caramel filling between cake layers.
2. Spread caramel over the top of the cake.
3. Frost the sides of the cake with buttercream frosting.
4. Press chopped pecans into the frosting on the sides.
5. Garnish the top with pecan halves.
Yield: One 3-layer cake.
